title: practical english usage - Part 57
content:
Somebody has paid for your meal. -» Your meal has been paid for.

Nobody listens to her. -> She is never listened to.

We have looked at the plan carefully. -> The plan has been looked at carefully.

The  objects of prepositional verbs can become subjects in passive structures.

1  The    plan     has    been      looked        at   carefully.

62 verbs     with    preposit     ions    in  the   passive      __ For more details of verbs with two objects, ►    8.

objects' in all grammars.

Note that prepositional phrases like 'to the police officer' are not called ‘ indirect a-meeling pkiee:) but not A meeting place was suggested to us. (          W^were-suggested explained ike-proMem:) but not The problem was explained to the children, (          The children were Explain (►   459) and suggest (►  598) cannot be used in structure A.

This watch was given (to) me by my father.

dropped before indirect object pronouns.

In structure B (e.g. The car was given to her sister), prepositions are sometimes How much  have you been offered?

We will never be told the real truth.

He was refused a visa because he had been in prison.

They are being paid a lot of money for doing very little.

The visitors were shown a collection of old manuscripts.

You were lent ten thousand pounds last year.

I've just been sent a whole lot of information.

Structure A  (e.g. Her sister was given the car) is the more common of the two.

said before, or on what needs to be put last in the sentence (►  67.2, 67.4).

The  choice between the two passive structures may depend on what has been Sam's photo had already been shown to the police officer The car was given to her sister.

B.  direct object becomes subject of passive verb The police officer had already been shown Sam's photo.

Her sister was given the car.

A.  indirect object becomes subject of passive verb Both of these structures can be made passive.

I had already shown Sam 's photo to the police officer.

She gave the car to her sister.

B. verb + direct object + preposition + indirect object I had already shown the police officer Sam's photo.

Passives      Section 6 grammar •  63   It was thought that..

it                                                           or   t NOT   is   started to make a profit.   I  is not expected to rain today.

However, most verbs cannot be used in this way.

We agreed to open a new branch. It was agreed to open a new branch.

They decided to meet at twelve. -> It was decided to meet at twelve.

be used in passive structures beginning with it.

A  few verbs that are followed by infinitives (for example decide, agree) can also 2 infinitive       objects:     They      decided         to   ...

It is said that his company is in trouble.

It was felt that he was the right man for the job.

It was thought that she was a spy.

However, passive structures are often possible with preparatory it (►   268).

Note that possessive nouns or pronouns cannot become passive subjects, either.
